model.name: LFM — Architecture Model
model.documentation: Architecture model for LFM (Looking-for-Mythic-Plus) — a Blazor WebAssembly + Azure Functions application that helps World of Warcraft players organise Mythic+ groups. Lifted from the repository's .NET solution, Bicep IaC, and GitHub Actions workflows. Scope is the production-facing runtime; test-only functions compiled behind #if E2E are intentionally excluded from the Application Interface inventory.

metadata.dc:title: LFM — Architecture Model
metadata.dc:creator: architecture-design 0.9.3
metadata.dc:description: LFM application architecture — Application, Technology, and Implementation & Migration layers extracted from source; Business, Motivation, and Strategy stubbed forward-only.

element.name: Lfm.App
element.documentation: Blazor WebAssembly SPA (Microsoft.NET.Sdk.BlazorWebAssembly, .NET 10). Hosted on Azure Static Web Apps. Source: app/Lfm.App.csproj.

element.name: Lfm.App.Core
element.documentation: Framework-neutral SPA service library (auth, i18n, services). Extracted so Stryker can mutate it. Source: app/Lfm.App.Core/Lfm.App.Core.csproj.

element.name: Lfm.Api
element.documentation: Azure Functions backend (Microsoft.Azure.Functions.Worker 2.52, isolated-worker, .NET 10). HTTP trigger + Timer trigger. Source: api/Lfm.Api.csproj, api/Program.cs.

element.name: Lfm.Contracts
element.documentation: Shared DTO library (records + FluentValidation rules) consumed by Lfm.App, Lfm.App.Core, and Lfm.Api. Source: shared/Lfm.Contracts/Lfm.Contracts.csproj.

element.name: Blizzard Battle.net
element.documentation: External — Blizzard's Battle.net OAuth 2.0 identity provider (battle.net). Consumed by Lfm.Api during the login flow. Source: api/Functions/BattleNetLoginFunction.cs, api/Functions/BattleNetCallbackFunction.cs, api/Services/BlizzardOAuthClient.cs.

element.name: Blizzard World of Warcraft API
element.documentation: External — Blizzard's WoW Profile API and Game Data API ({region}.api.blizzard.com). Consumed by Lfm.Api for character data and reference data. Source: api/Services/BlizzardProfileClient.cs, api/Services/BlizzardGameDataClient.cs.

element.name: Blizzard Render CDN
element.documentation: External — render.worldofwarcraft.com character avatar / item icon CDN. Consumed directly by the SPA browser (allow-listed in CSP img-src). Source: app/wwwroot/staticwebapp.config.json.template (Content-Security-Policy).

element.name: LFM (internal)
element.documentation: Trust boundary — code authored, deployed, and operated by the LFM project (Blazor SPA + Functions API + shared contracts). All elements inside this Grouping run on Azure resources owned by the LFM subscription; the boundary marks the perimeter for AGPL-3.0 disclosure obligations and for security-incident scope.

element.name: Blizzard (external)
element.documentation: Trust boundary — third-party services owned and operated by Blizzard Entertainment (Battle.net OAuth identity provider, World of Warcraft Profile / Game Data APIs, render.worldofwarcraft.com CDN). LFM has zero operational control over these; outages, breaking changes, and rate-limit policy are external risks.

element.name: SPA UI
element.documentation: Browser-facing Blazor WASM UI surface — pages, components, navigation. Realised by Lfm.App. Source: app/Pages, app/Layout.

element.name: REST API (/api/*)
element.documentation: Production-facing HTTP/JSON API surface exposed by Lfm.Api with route prefix /api (api/host.json). Aggregates 30+ endpoint functions: me, me-update, me-delete, guild-get, guild-update, guild-admin-get, runs-create/list/detail/update/delete/signup/cancel-signup, raider-character/-add/-enrich, raider-cleanup, battlenet-login/-callback/-logout/-characters/-characters-refresh/-character-portraits, wow-reference-instances/-expansions/-specializations/-refresh, privacy-email, health/-ready, cors-preflight catch-all OPTIONS, plus -v1 aliases where applicable. Excludes test-only functions compiled behind #if E2E, such as /api/e2e/login. Source: api/Functions/*.cs.

element.name: Battle.net OAuth 2.0
element.documentation: OAuth 2.0 authorization-code endpoint at https://{region}.battle.net/oauth/. Realised by Blizzard Battle.net. Source: api/Services/BlizzardOAuthClient.cs.

element.name: WoW Profile API
element.documentation: Per-user character profile endpoints at https://{region}.api.blizzard.com/profile/. Realised by Blizzard World of Warcraft API. Source: api/Services/BlizzardProfileClient.cs.

element.name: WoW Game Data API
element.documentation: Static reference data endpoints at https://{region}.api.blizzard.com/data/wow/. Realised by Blizzard World of Warcraft API. Used by the weekly reference refresh. Source: api/Services/BlizzardGameDataClient.cs, api/Functions/WowReferenceRefreshTimerFunction.cs.

element.name: Character render images
element.documentation: Static image URLs at https://render.worldofwarcraft.com/. Fetched directly by the SPA browser (allow-listed in CSP img-src). Source: app/wwwroot/staticwebapp.config.json.template.

element.name: Authentication & Session
element.documentation: Session-cookie-based authentication via Battle.net OAuth. Source: api/Functions/BattleNetLoginFunction.cs, BattleNetCallbackFunction.cs, BattleNetLogoutFunction.cs, api/Auth/*.

element.name: Account Self-Service
element.documentation: Read / update / delete the current user account. Source: api/Functions/MeFunction.cs, MeUpdateFunction.cs, MeDeleteFunction.cs.

element.name: Guild Management
element.documentation: Read / update guild metadata; admin-only roster operations. Source: api/Functions/GuildFunction.cs, GuildAdminFunction.cs.

element.name: Mythic+ Runs
element.documentation: Create / list / read / update / delete Mythic+ runs and signup / cancel-signup operations. Source: api/Functions/RunsCreateFunction.cs, RunsListFunction.cs, RunsDetailFunction.cs, RunsUpdateFunction.cs, RunsDeleteFunction.cs, RunsSignupFunction.cs, RunsCancelSignupFunction.cs, RunsMigrateSchemaFunction.cs.

element.name: Raider Characters
element.documentation: Add a character to the user's raider profile, enrich it with Blizzard data, and clean up stale records on a timer. Source: api/Functions/RaiderCharacterFunction.cs, RaiderCharacterAddFunction.cs, RaiderCharacterEnrichFunction.cs, RaiderCleanupFunction.cs.

element.name: Battle.net Characters
element.documentation: Browse the current user's Blizzard account profile (character list) and refresh portraits. Source: api/Functions/BattleNetCharactersFunction.cs, BattleNetCharactersRefreshFunction.cs, BattleNetCharacterPortraitsFunction.cs.

element.name: WoW Reference Data
element.documentation: Static Blizzard reference data (instances, expansions, specializations, hero talent trees). Read-from-blob endpoints plus an admin/timer-driven refresh that fetches from the WoW Game Data API. Source: api/Functions/WowReferenceInstancesFunction.cs, WowReferenceExpansionsFunction.cs, WowReferenceSpecializationsFunction.cs, WowReferenceRefreshFunction.cs, WowReferenceRefreshTimerFunction.cs.

element.name: Health Probe
element.documentation: Liveness and readiness probes for the Function App. Source: api/Functions/HealthFunction.cs, PrivacyContactFunction.cs.

element.name: app/Pages/RunsPage.razor
element.documentation: Blazor page component — renders the open-runs list at /runs and the per-run detail (with signup affordance) at /runs/{RunId}. Source: app/Pages/RunsPage.razor.

element.name: /runs
element.documentation: Blazor `@page "/runs"` route on RunsPage.razor — list view of open Mythic+ runs.

element.name: /runs/{RunId}
element.documentation: Blazor `@page "/runs/{RunId}"` route on RunsPage.razor — detail view of a single run; carries the signup affordance for user-driven Sign up for run.

element.name: Azure Static Web Apps
element.documentation: Microsoft.Web/staticSites — Free tier. Hosts the published Lfm.App Blazor WASM bundle. Source: infra/modules/swa.bicep.

element.name: Azure Function App
element.documentation: Microsoft.Web/sites (kind=functionapp,linux) hosting Lfm.Api. System-assigned managed identity grants data-plane RBAC to Cosmos, Storage, and Key Vault. Source: infra/modules/functions.bicep.

element.name: App Service Plan
element.documentation: Microsoft.Web/serverfarms — compute substrate for the Function App. Current committed Bicep uses Y1 Dynamic (Linux Consumption); deploy-infra.yml carries a one-time cleanup path for earlier Flex Consumption resources. Source: infra/modules/functions.bicep (hostingPlan), .github/workflows/deploy-infra.yml.

element.name: Azure Functions runtime v4 (.NET 10 isolated-worker)
element.documentation: FUNCTIONS_EXTENSION_VERSION=~4, FUNCTIONS_WORKER_RUNTIME=dotnet-isolated. Routes prefix /api per api/host.json. Source: api/host.json, infra/modules/functions.bicep (app settings).

element.name: Azure Cosmos DB account
element.documentation: Microsoft.DocumentDB/databaseAccounts — SQL API, Free Tier, single-write region, disableLocalAuth=true. CanNotDelete lock + diagnostic settings to Log Analytics. Source: infra/modules/cosmos.bicep.

element.name: Azure Storage account
element.documentation: Microsoft.Storage/storageAccounts — Standard_LRS, allowSharedKeyAccess=false, TLS 1.2 minimum. CanNotDelete lock + diagnostic settings. Source: infra/modules/storage.bicep.

element.name: Azure Key Vault
element.documentation: Microsoft.KeyVault/vaults — RBAC-authorized, soft-delete + purge protection. Holds the Data Protection key. CanNotDelete lock + diagnostic settings. Source: infra/modules/keyvault.bicep.

element.name: Application Insights
element.documentation: Microsoft.Insights/components — workspace-based, paired with Log Analytics. Receives telemetry from Lfm.Api via Microsoft.Azure.Functions.Worker.ApplicationInsights. Source: infra/modules/functions.bicep (appInsights).

element.name: Log Analytics workspace
element.documentation: Microsoft.OperationalInsights/workspaces — central log sink for Cosmos, Storage, Key Vault, Function App, and SWA diagnostic settings; backs the workspace-based Application Insights. Source: infra/modules/loganalytics.bicep.

element.name: Action Group + Cosmos throttle alert
element.documentation: Microsoft.Insights/actionGroups + Microsoft.Insights/metricAlerts — emails the privacy contact when Cosmos throttles (TotalRequests with StatusCode=429, autoMitigate=true). Source: infra/modules/alerts.bicep.

element.name: Function App MI
element.documentation: System-assigned managed identity on the Function App (`identity: { type: 'SystemAssigned' }`). This is modelled as a Technology Service to make the Technology Security view show the RBAC-authorised access surface; it is not a standalone Azure resource. Holds the data-plane RBAC role assignments that authorise every outbound call from Lfm.Api: Key Vault Secrets User on the Vault, SQL Role 00000000-...-00000002 (Cosmos DB Built-in Data Contributor) on the Cosmos account, Storage Blob Data Owner / Queue Data Contributor / Table Data Contributor on the Storage account, Monitoring Metrics Publisher on App Insights (required because Application Insights has DisableLocalAuth=true), plus Data Protection-specific Key Vault Crypto User and Storage Blob Data Contributor grants. Source: infra/modules/functions.bicep (kvRoleAssignment, cosmosRoleAssignment, storageBlobRole, storageQueueRole, storageTableRole, appInsightsRoleAssignment), infra/modules/dataprotection.bicep (kvCryptoUserRoleAssignment, storageBlobContributorRoleAssignment).

element.name: Cosmos SQL database
element.documentation: Parameterized Cosmos DB SQL database (`databaseName`, supplied from the deploy workflow's COSMOS_DATABASE repo variable). Parent for all dynamic-data containers. Source: infra/modules/cosmos.bicep (database), infra/main.bicep.

element.name: Cosmos container: raiders
element.documentation: Per-user raider character documents. Source: infra/modules/cosmos.bicep (raidersContainer).

element.name: Cosmos container: runs
element.documentation: Mythic+ run documents (signups, schedule, status). Source: infra/modules/cosmos.bicep (runsContainer).

element.name: Cosmos container: guilds
element.documentation: Guild metadata + roster cache. Source: infra/modules/cosmos.bicep (guildsContainer).

element.name: Cosmos container: idempotency
element.documentation: Idempotency-Key replay store, with per-document TTL. Source: infra/modules/cosmos.bicep (idempotencyContainer), api/Services/IdempotencyStore.cs.

element.name: Blob container: wow
element.documentation: Static Blizzard reference data (journal-instance, playable-specialization, playable-class, playable-race, hero-talent-tree). Source: infra/modules/storage.bicep (wowContainer), docs/storage-architecture.md.

element.name: Blob container: dataprotection
element.documentation: ASP.NET Core Data Protection key ring (encrypted at rest by Key Vault key). Source: infra/modules/storage.bicep (dataProtectionContainer), api/Program.cs (PersistKeysToAzureBlobStorage).

element.name: Blob container: deployments
element.documentation: Functions deployment package staging (linked via WEBSITE_RUN_FROM_PACKAGE). Source: infra/modules/storage.bicep (deploymentContainer).

element.name: KV key: data-protection-key
element.documentation: Versionless RSA key wrapping the Data Protection ring (auto-rotated). Source: infra/modules/keyvault.bicep (dataProtectionKey).

element.name: CI
element.documentation: Build, format check, and unit test gate on every push and PR. Source: .github/workflows/ci.yml.

element.name: Deploy (orchestrator)
element.documentation: Top-level release orchestrator chaining infra and app workflows. Source: .github/workflows/deploy.yml.

element.name: Deploy Infrastructure
element.documentation: Bicep what-if + deployment to the target resource group, plus post-deployment alert wiring. Source: .github/workflows/deploy-infra.yml.

element.name: Deploy App — Build
element.documentation: Builds and packages the Functions zip and the Blazor WASM static bundle as workflow artifacts. Source: .github/workflows/deploy-app-build.yml.

element.name: Deploy App
element.documentation: Downloads the built artifacts, applies env-var substitution, and deploys to Function App + Static Web App. Source: .github/workflows/deploy-app.yml.

element.name: E2E
element.documentation: End-to-end Playwright .NET tests against the in-process stack (Cosmos emulator + Azurite + Functions host + Kestrel). Source: .github/workflows/e2e.yml.

element.name: Analyze Infrastructure (PSRule)
element.documentation: PSRule for Azure WAF compliance check on every infra/* change. Source: .github/workflows/analyze-infra.yml, ps-rule.yaml.

element.name: Secret Scanning (Gitleaks)
element.documentation: Required PR check for committed secrets, chained into deploy.yml via needs:. Source: .github/workflows/secrets-scan.yml.

element.name: License Compliance
element.documentation: AGPL-compatibility check for transitive dependency licenses. Source: .github/workflows/license-compliance.yml, .github/workflows/dep-license-check.yml.

element.name: Stryker Nightly
element.documentation: Nightly mutation testing against Lfm.App.Core. Source: .github/workflows/stryker-nightly.yml.

element.name: Dependabot Auto-Merge
element.documentation: Auto-merges Dependabot PRs once required checks pass. Source: .github/workflows/dependabot-auto-merge.yml.

element.name: Bicep infrastructure
element.documentation: Output of Deploy Infrastructure — the deployed resource group containing all Technology Layer nodes. Source: infra/main.bicep applied via .github/workflows/deploy-infra.yml.

element.name: Functions deployment package
element.documentation: Zipped Lfm.Api publish artifact (including resilience + DP wiring) deployed via WEBSITE_RUN_FROM_PACKAGE. Source: deploy-app-build.yml → deploy-app.yml.

element.name: Blazor WASM static bundle
element.documentation: Published Lfm.App wwwroot (with templated security.txt + staticwebapp.config.json + index.html) uploaded to Azure Static Web Apps. Source: deploy-app-build.yml → deploy-app.yml.

element.name: Dev
element.documentation: FORWARD-ONLY stub — a Dev environment does not currently exist in this repo. The `deploy.yml` / `deploy-infra.yml` / `deploy-app.yml` workflows tag every deployment with `environment=production` and there are no environment-aware GitHub variables for any other target. Architect to confirm whether a Dev plateau will be introduced or whether this stub should be removed.

element.name: Staging
element.documentation: FORWARD-ONLY stub — a Staging environment does not currently exist in this repo. See Dev plateau for context.

element.name: Repository baseline
element.documentation: Baseline Plateau for the Migration view: committed source, Bicep, and workflow definitions before the production deploy applies them.

element.name: Release to production
element.documentation: Migration Gap closed by the deploy workflows when current source and infrastructure definitions become the Production Plateau.

element.name: Deploy to Production
element.documentation: Implementation Event representing a successful production deployment after Deploy Infrastructure and Deploy App complete.

element.name: Production
element.documentation: The single live environment. `deploy.yml` deploys infra + app to this resource group on every relevant push to main, gated by a workflow-level `concurrency: deploy` lock to serialise concurrent runs. Resources are tagged `environment=production`. Source: .github/workflows/deploy.yml + deploy-infra.yml + deploy-app.yml.

element.name: Find a group for Mythic+
element.documentation: FORWARD-ONLY stub. Inferred from runs / signup application services. Architect to confirm framing.

element.name: Guild roster management
element.documentation: FORWARD-ONLY stub. Inferred from guild application services. Architect to confirm framing.

element.name: Personal character portfolio
element.documentation: FORWARD-ONLY stub. Inferred from raider-character + battlenet-characters services. Architect to confirm framing.

element.name: Group-finding service
element.documentation: FORWARD-ONLY stub. Inferred from id-svc-runs. Architect to confirm framing and add the realising Business Process / Function.

element.name: Guild roster service
element.documentation: FORWARD-ONLY stub. Inferred from id-svc-guild. Architect to confirm framing.

element.name: Character-management service
element.documentation: FORWARD-ONLY stub. Inferred from id-svc-raiders + id-svc-bnet-chars. Architect to confirm framing.

element.name: Account-management service
element.documentation: FORWARD-ONLY stub. Inferred from id-svc-me + id-svc-auth. Architect to confirm framing.

element.name: Mythic+ Player
element.documentation: FORWARD-ONLY stub. A WoW player using LFM to find a group for a Mythic+ run. Inferred from the public LFM use-case surface; not validated against business stakeholders.

element.name: Player wants to run a key
element.documentation: FORWARD-ONLY stub. Entry Event for the run-signup chain — the player decides they want to do a Mythic+ run. Out-of-band; no system trigger.

element.name: Browse open runs
element.documentation: FORWARD-ONLY stub. The player loads /runs and reviews the list of currently-open Mythic+ runs. Realised by the Mythic+ Runs application service (RunsListFunction).

element.name: Inspect run detail
element.documentation: FORWARD-ONLY stub. The player drills into /runs/{RunId} to read the run's specifics (key level, dungeon, scheduled time, party so far, leader notes). Realised by the Mythic+ Runs application service (RunsDetailFunction).

element.name: Sign up for run
element.documentation: FORWARD-ONLY stub. The player picks a character + role and submits a signup. Realised by the Mythic+ Runs application service (RunsSignupFunction). User-driven — the §9.3 drill-down shows the UI entry point at /runs/{RunId}.

element.name: Cancel signup
element.documentation: FORWARD-ONLY stub. Alternative path — the player removes themselves from the run. Realised by the Mythic+ Runs application service (RunsCancelSignupFunction).

element.name: Signup confirmed
element.documentation: FORWARD-ONLY stub. Terminal outcome of the signup chain — the run leader sees the signup, the player has secured a slot.

element.name: Run
element.documentation: FORWARD-ONLY stub. Business representation of a Mythic+ run (key level, dungeon, scheduled time, party slots). Realised by the Cosmos `runs` container.

element.name: Signup
element.documentation: FORWARD-ONLY stub. Business representation of a player's signup to a run — character, role, status. Embedded inside the Run document in the Cosmos `runs` container.

element.name: Mythic+ group-finding friction
element.documentation: FORWARD-ONLY stub. Inferred from the existence of a Mythic+ group-finding application surface. Architect to confirm or replace with the real driver.

element.name: Frictionless raid signup
element.documentation: FORWARD-ONLY stub. Inferred from runs-signup / runs-cancel-signup endpoints. Architect to confirm.

element.name: Expose run signup entry point
element.documentation: FORWARD-ONLY requirement stub tying the run-signup goal to an architectural decision: keep a discoverable UI route and API-backed application service for viewing a run and submitting or cancelling a signup.

view.name: View: Application Cooperation
view.documentation: Application Layer overview with explicit trust boundary. The LFM (internal) Grouping holds the four code projects we own and operate (Lfm.App, Lfm.App.Core, Lfm.Api, Lfm.Contracts); Lfm.Contracts is the shared wire-DTO library used by all three. The Blizzard (external) Grouping holds the third-party services we consume (Battle.net OAuth identity provider, World of Warcraft Profile + Game Data APIs); we have no operational control over them. Each Application Interface is nested inside its owning Component (Composition edges hidden per AD-L7); the only Serving edges drawn cross the boundaries that matter — Lfm.App.Core into Lfm.App, REST API into Lfm.App, Battle.net OAuth into Lfm.Api, and the two WoW APIs into Lfm.Api. Lfm.Contracts' shared use is shown by Composition into the Grouping (members are inferred to wire through it).

view.name: View: Technology Realisation (Hosting + Data Plane)
view.documentation: Azure Technology Layer hosting + data-plane overview. Static Web Apps hosts Lfm.App; Function App hosts Lfm.Api; the data plane is Cosmos DB (Free Tier, single-region) + Storage (Standard_LRS) + Key Vault; the workspace-based observability stack (Log Analytics aggregates Application Insights) receives telemetry from the Function App. **The data-plane RBAC story (system-assigned MI, role assignments, Serving dependencies into Function App) is in the Technology Security view (sibling).** Diagnostic settings (Cosmos / Storage / KV / SWA / Function App → Log Analytics) and the Action Group + Cosmos throttle alert are tracked in <relationships> but reserved for an Observability detail view. App-Service Plan SKU and runtime version are in <elements> documentation. Region is `vars.AZURE_LOCATION` from the deploy workflow — single-region, no failover.

view.name: View: Technology Security (Managed Identity + Data-plane RBAC)
view.documentation: How Lfm.Api authenticates to every data-plane resource. The Function App carries a system-assigned managed identity (no client secrets, no shared keys). The MI is granted six data-plane RBAC roles in `infra/modules/functions.bicep`: Key Vault Secrets User on the Vault (Read), Cosmos DB Built-in Data Contributor (RW) on the account, Storage Blob Data Owner + Queue Data Contributor + Table Data Contributor (RW) on the account, Monitoring Metrics Publisher (Write) on App Insights — the last is required because Application Insights has DisableLocalAuth=true. `infra/modules/dataprotection.bicep` adds Data Protection-specific Key Vault Crypto User (wrap/unwrap) and Storage Blob Data Contributor on the `dataprotection` container. The Cosmos account itself sets disableLocalAuth=true and the Storage account sets allowSharedKeyAccess=false, so RBAC is the only authentication path. Reading this view: each Serving edge into the Function App is a runtime dependency whose access is authorised by the Function App MI's documented role assignments.

view.name: View: Production Release Migration
view.documentation: Single-environment Migration view lifted from `.github/workflows/`. LFM deploys to one environment ("production"); deploy.yml has no environment input and tags every resource `environment=production`. The release path shows a Repository baseline Plateau, the Release to production Gap, and the Deploy to Production Implementation Event that reaches the Production Plateau. The Deploy orchestrator aggregates Deploy Infrastructure (Bicep what-if + apply), Deploy App-Build (Functions zip + Blazor wwwroot bundle), and Deploy App (uploads to Function App + SWA). Dev / Staging Plateaus exist as FORWARD-ONLY stubs in <elements> for future multi-environment work but are not drawn here — the current architecture is single-environment.

view.name: View: Capability Map (FORWARD-ONLY scaffolding)
view.documentation: Strategy + Business + Application realisation scaffold to seed architect work. The three Strategy Capabilities ("Find a group for Mythic+", "Guild roster management", "Personal character portfolio") are FORWARD-ONLY suggestions inferred from the existing Application Service surface — they have not been validated against business stakeholders. Below each Capability sits a FORWARD-ONLY Business Service that realises it; below each Business Service sit the **real** Application Services from Lfm.Api that realise it. The chain is therefore: real ApplicationService (bottom, lifted from code) → forward-only BusinessService (middle, architect to confirm) → forward-only Capability (top, architect to confirm). Architect's task: validate or rename the Capabilities, validate or split the Business Services, then add Drivers / Goals / Outcomes / Stakeholders in the Motivation Layer to anchor the "why."

view.name: View: Motivation (FORWARD-ONLY scaffolding)
view.documentation: FORWARD-ONLY — architect-authored Motivation view for the run-signup capability. The Driver and Goal are inferred from the Mythic+ signup surface and must be validated or replaced by the architect.

view.name: View: Mythic+ Run Signup — Business Process Cooperation
view.documentation: FORWARD-ONLY — architect-authored. Business Layer process-flow for the user-driven "Sign up for Mythic+ run" use case. Layout follows the §9.7 specialisation: Active structure (Mythic+ Player Actor) above, Behaviour (Triggering chain Event → Browse → Inspect → Sign up → confirmed; Flow alt-path to Cancel signup) middle, Passive structure (Run / Signup Business Objects) below. FORWARD-ONLY status is kept in element documentation rather than visible labels; names and the chain shape are inferred from the API surface (api/Functions/Runs*Function.cs) and the Blazor pages (app/Pages/RunsPage.razor at /runs and /runs/{RunId}) — they have not been validated against business stakeholders. The drill-down into Application + Technology realising each step is represented by sibling §9.3 Service Realization views.

view.name: View: Browse open runs — Service Realization (Process-rooted)
view.documentation: Cooperation view: id-view-business-processes. FORWARD-ONLY (Business Layer top) drilling into the real Application + Technology realisation chain for "Browse open runs". The user-driven entry point is the UI Application Component `app/Pages/RunsPage.razor` exposing the `/runs` Application Interface.

view.name: View: Inspect run detail — Service Realization (Process-rooted)
view.documentation: Cooperation view: id-view-business-processes. FORWARD-ONLY (Business Layer top) drilling into the real Application + Technology realisation chain for "Inspect run detail". The user-driven entry point is the UI Application Component `app/Pages/RunsPage.razor` exposing the `/runs/{RunId}` Application Interface.

view.name: View: Sign up for run — Service Realization (Process-rooted)
view.documentation: Cooperation view: id-view-business-processes. FORWARD-ONLY (Business Layer top) drilling into a real Application + Technology realisation chain for "Sign up for Mythic+ run". Process-rooted §9.3 modality: the Business Process at the top is realised by an Application Service (Mythic+ Runs) which is itself realised by an Application Component (Lfm.Api) Assigned to a Technology Node (Azure Function App), with Cosmos DB as a data-plane Serving relationship. The user-driven entry point (per §9.3 AD-B-10 + Blazor idiom) is the UI Application Component `app/Pages/RunsPage.razor` exposing the `/runs/{RunId}` Application Interface; the UI Component carries a Realisation edge to the Business Process. Architect's task: validate the framing of the Business Process and the Realisation chain.

view.name: View: Cancel signup — Service Realization (Process-rooted)
view.documentation: Cooperation view: id-view-business-processes. FORWARD-ONLY (Business Layer top) drilling into the real Application + Technology realisation chain for "Cancel signup". The user-driven entry point is the UI Application Component `app/Pages/RunsPage.razor` exposing the `/runs/{RunId}` Application Interface.
